title: grammitis flagellata
intro: Grammitis flagellata, also known as the whip fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with studies indicating that it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Grammitis flagellata include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, often made into a tea or infusion, are used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
